rows.notes: Clear page title / heading at the top of the page. No monetary amounts present; aligned as a centered header.
The description is legible in dark ink; the pounds column clearly shows '90' aligned with the pounds line and the s/d columns are '0 0'. Vertical ruling and alignment are unambiguous.

Pounds (25) and shillings (17) are clearly aligned. Pence reads as '10' but there is a small trailing loop/mark after the 10 that resembles the 'ob' (halfpenny) sign; interpretation as 0.5 (halfpenny) is plausible but slightly ambiguous due to ink flourish.
Clear reading. Pounds 20, shillings 8, pence 0 are well aligned in their respective columns and digits are crisp.
Entries and amounts are clearly written; pounds '58' is unambiguous and s/d read as zeros.

rows.description: Cuddington Reet. pro Triennio cum dimidio
rows.notes: Description has some minor abbreviation/Latin ('cum dimidio') but amounts read clearly as 70/0/0 with good alignment to the pounds/shillings/pence columns.

rows.description: Yardingdon pro Quinquennio a pr Man ad Mie:
rows.notes: Description is somewhat cramped and contains several abbreviations; pounds column clearly '50'. Shillings/pence are both '0'. Slight uncertainty about exact spelling of place name, but monetary columns are clear.

Place and Latin suffixes partly abbreviated but readable. Pounds and shillings (14/10) align cleanly. Pence column shows very small mark but consistent with zero; chosen as 0 with moderate confidence.
Amounts read as 5/9/7; pence digit is slightly narrow but fits within the pence column. Description legible.

The description line has several corrections/overwrites but the pounds column shows '55' clearly. S and d columns read as zeros though there is ink crossing in the description making the row less clean.

rows.description: The Dult Rent pro Anno cum Arrear. Tinnes
rows.notes: Description is heavily abbreviated and there is some overwriting, but the numeric columns align to read 7/5/6. Moderate ambiguity due to ink and abbreviation in description.
Text is legible; amounts 3/17/6 are clear and digits are well placed in columns. Good contrast and alignment.
Description and small amounts legible but cramped. Pounds appear blank/zero; shillings '2' and pence '6' read from the correct columns though the leading pounds space is faint.

rows.description: Bugg pro anno cum Arrear. sup anni
rows.notes: Pounds and shillings digits are slightly cramped but readable as 7/11; pence looks like '9'. There is some ink blotting near the description which slightly reduces confidence.
Clear entry: name readable and numeric columns show 2/12/8 with good alignment. No unusual marks.
Pounds '63' is clear. Shillings read as '1', pence as '10'. The pence '10' is a two-digit figure in a narrow pence column but aligns with the pence ruling; moderate confidence.
Simple, clear entry with amounts 8/0/0; handwriting crisp and alignment correct.
Place name and '19 Anni' legible. Amounts read as 9/10/0 with clear alignment; shillings (10) within valid range.
Pounds space appears blank/zero; shillings '16' are clear. Pence column blank for 0. Good column alignment.

rows.description: Greenway alias Aud. Oxon: pro Octen. cum dim.
rows.notes: Description contains several abbreviations but numeric columns read 8/10/0. Slight uncertainty in some letters of the place name, but the amounts align cleanly.
Short, clear entry and amounts 1/0/0 are unambiguous.
Amounts read clearly as 1/3/4; description is short and legible.
Pounds blank/zero, shillings 10 visible, pence 0. Clear column alignment.
Description is somewhat faint (reads 'Sader' or similar). Numeric columns appear to be 4/2/4 but the first digits are light and require cautious reading.
Pounds look like '26'. Shillings '6' and pence '4' appear to align, but the description and some digits are lightly written; moderate confidence.
Amounts read 1/13/9 with clear digits for shillings and pence. Description legible.

The entry contains abbreviation 'Quadrien' and 'cum dim.' The numeric line reads 3/6/6 but there is a small scratch through the pence area; chosen reading matches column alignment though slightly uncertain.
Pounds and shillings (1/18) are legible. Pence reads as '10' with a small trailing mark that could be 'ob' (halfpenny). The tail is faint; interpreted as 0.5 halfpenny with moderate confidence.
Clear reading: 4/2/10; digits occupy their columns and are easily read.
Strong, clear figures 24/15/11. Good contrast and alignment; description legible.
Pounds and shillings legible as 7/2; pence '2' visible though slightly narrow. Overall readable.
Description contains abbreviation 'Ten:' but digits 2/13/9 align well in the monetary columns and are clear.
Short entry and amounts 6/0/0 are clear and unambiguous.

The place name is legible. The shillings column for this row is crowded; read as '16' though there is slight smearing where the digit was written. Pence reads '8'. Chosen values are internally consistent but with moderate uncertainty.

rows.description: Lade Lane sparkes of Merton ad mie
rows.notes: Description is abbreviated and runs into the margin. Numeric columns read similarly to the previous row (8/16/8). The ink is slightly faded here, causing moderate uncertainty in the shillings reading.

rows.description: Medenham pro Anno ad Fest. Sandi. Tho: pro divy.
rows.notes: Long abbreviated description with several clerical contractions; amounts look like 6/13/4 but the description ink is light and cramped so reading carries moderate uncertainty.
Entry and amounts '8/0/0' are clearly written. The place name has a flourish but remains legible.
Final visible line on the page. Amounts read clearly as 4/0/0 and description is short and legible.
